name: Standard Base
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Standard Base recipe, posted 2016-05-25, visited 2026-05-7. Source: https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/. 'Whole Milk' is quoted as 3.3%, which I assume is 3.25% rounded up, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. 'Heavy Cream' is quoted as 36%, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. The 'Egg Yolk' amount is a whole multiple of 18g large egg yolks. The stabilizers are the 4:2:1 'Underbelly General Purpose Stabilizer Blend', total 1.4g. The 15g of 'Invert Syrup' can be replaced by 6g each of 'Dextrose' and 'Fructose', as per https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/#comment-12, which is much easier to work with.

name: French Variation
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's French Variation recipe, posted 2016-05-25, visited 2026-05-7. Source: https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/. 'Whole Milk' is quoted as 3.3%, which I assume is 3.25% rounded up, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. 'Heavy Cream' is quoted as 36%, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. The 'Egg Yolk' amount is a whole multiple of 18g large egg yolks. The stabilizers are the 4:2:1 'Underbelly General Purpose Stabilizer Blend', total 1.05g. The 15g of 'Invert Syrup' can be replaced by 6g each of 'Dextrose' and 'Fructose', as per https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/#comment-12, which is much easier to work with.

name: Light Variation
author: Underbelly
comments: Underbelly's Light Variation recipe, posted 2016-05-25, visited 2026-05-7. Source: https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/. 'Whole Milk' is quoted as 3.3%, which I assume is 3.25% rounded up, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. 'Heavy Cream' is quoted as 36%, consistent with Sci-Cream's definition. The stabilizers are a 4:3:2 blend, total 1.8g, and 2g of 'Lecithin'. This is inconsistent with 'Underbelly Eggless Ice Cream Stabilizer Blend', which uses a 4:2:1 blend, and 4 'Lecithin'. It's also inconsistent with the 2.1g of stabilizers in the composition table, and with the stated 4.5g of 'Lecithin' in the discussion. The stabilizer blends were both ostensibly edited on 2018-10, but they are inconsistent with each other. The 15g of 'Invert Syrup' can be replaced by 6g each of 'Dextrose' and 'Fructose', as per https://under-belly.org/basic-ice-cream-recipe-examples/#comment-12, which is much easier to work with.
